danger_signals.visual_form: Variety of hostile entity types (organic mutants, mechanical enemies, cyborgs)
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring combat or evasion; different enemy types signal different danger contexts

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual wear state on equipped weapons; deteriorating condition indicator
danger_signals.meaning: Resources depleting; danger of being under-armed; need for maintenance or weapon replacement

danger_signals.visual_form: HUD display showing health, psionic points, and nanites at bottom left of interface
danger_signals.meaning: Player vulnerability state; low resources indicate increased danger from encounters

danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental degradation, mutated enemy forms, corpses and remains
danger_signals.meaning: Systemic threat state; the ship is compromised; widespread danger across all areas

danger_signals.visual_form: Keypads requiring hacks, sealed doors, restricted access points
danger_signals.meaning: Containment zones; danger ahead or danger being contained; resource/tactical barriers

safety_signals.visual_form: Interactive machines dispensing ammunition, health packs, supplies
safety_signals.meaning: Resource replenishment points; safe zones for inventory management and recovery

safety_signals.visual_form: Specialized recovery stations placed throughout ship areas
safety_signals.meaning: Death checkpoint; safe respawn mechanism within local area; indicates controlled zone

safety_signals.visual_form: Sections of ship with defeated enemies and looted supplies
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary safety; reduced immediate threat level in that zone
Information gathering opportunities; narrative safe spaces explaining world context

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Three branches: Marine, Navy, OSA (Officer of Psionic Affairs)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Player can select service branch at start; different starting loadouts and visual skill sets indicate faction origin
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Military equipment, keypads, standard weaponry, cyber-upgrade units throughout ship representing UNN infrastructure

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Genetic infection outbreak markers; visually distinct from military/mechanical entities
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Organic mutants as opposed to mechanical enemies; different visual form from baseline human/military aesthetic
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Corpses, biological contamination signs, areas of genetic degradation on the starship

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Protocol droids, robots, cyborgs, mechanical systems
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Mechanical vs organic distinction; robots and cyborgs represent automated/corporate control systems
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Hacking terminals, cyber-upgrade units, mechanical doors and locks, energy weapon caches

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Audio logs and ghostly apparitions explaining prior ship functionality and military operations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Narrative exposition through discovered recordings; reveals what ship was before catastrophe

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mutated enemies present throughout ship; biological contamination visual markers; corpses and remains visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Hostile organic entities attack on sight; environment shows signs of biological degradation; emergency protocols triggered

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Limited ammunition types scattered throughout; vending machines offer limited supplies; weapon degradation visible on equipped items
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must carefully choose which ammunition type to use against specific enemies; rationing behavior required

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sealed doors, hacking-required keypads, restricted access areas, cyber-locked vending machines
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Navigation blocked; player must hack or find alternate routes; tactical puzzle-solving required to progress

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: HUD display prominent showing depleted health, low psionic points, reduced nanite currency
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased evasion; seeking recovery machines; prioritizing resource gathering; tactical retreat patterns

environmental_storytelling.example: Backstory progressively explained through player acquisition of audio recordings; explains ship history, outbreak origin, character deaths
Encounters with spectral figures that provide narrative context and emotional weight to disaster scenario

environmental_storytelling.technique: Enemy type distribution as threat mapping
environmental_storytelling.example: Different areas contain different enemy compositions (organic mutants vs mechanical vs cyborgs), revealing containment strategies or infection spread patterns

environmental_storytelling.technique: Weapon and ammunition scarcity as survival pressure
environmental_storytelling.example: Limited ammunition scattered throughout ship; specific ammo types required for specific enemies; visual depletion drives narrative tension

environmental_storytelling.technique: Locked areas and hacking requirements as infrastructure storytelling
environmental_storytelling.example: Keypads and sealed doors indicate former security measures; hacking nodes tell story of ship compartmentalization and emergency protocols

environmental_storytelling.technique: Corpses and remains as visual evidence
environmental_storytelling.example: Bodies throughout ship environments; decay states and injury patterns suggest what happened in each area

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Attack player on sight; represent primary infection threat; vulnerable to anti-personnel ammunition
Execute security protocols; vulnerable to armor-piercing and energy weapons; represent corporate/automated control systems
Encounter with player visible in interface mode; may be hostile or informational depending on context
Seeks resources (ammunition, health packs, nanites); activates vending machines and reconstruction machines; repairs degraded weapons; hacks restricted systems
Sealed doors, lockdowns, emergency protocols; vending machines offer limited supplies; cyber-upgrade units provide character advancement resource

notes: System Shock 2 communicates world-state primarily through resource availability, enemy type distribution, environmental hazards, and narrative exposition via audio logs. The cyberpunk starship setting in 2114 creates a political context of military organization (UNN) vs organic infection outbreak vs mechanical/corporate systems. Danger is signaled through enemy presence, resource depletion, and containment breaches. Safety is indicated by recovery stations and cleared areas. The game uses visual degradation (weapon wear, health indicators) and environmental design (locked areas, contamination markers) to reinforce the catastrophic world-state. Role-playing elements (skill trees, career selection) allow player customization within faction-defined contexts, reflecting military hierarchy and organizational structure. Scarcity mechanics create emergent narrative through resource management—different ammunition types for different enemies functionally communicate ecosystem relationships and threat hierarchies.
